get.description: List all quarantined objects matching the criteria in the filter.

parameters.name: filter.cloudOfficeTenantUuid
parameters.description:
Reference to [cloud office tenant].

If filled, only objects of the referred tenant are returned. If empty or null, objects of any tenant are returned.

type: cloud_office_protection.v1.CloudOfficeTenant

parameters.name: filter.emailInternetMessageId
parameters.description:
If filled, only messages with this Message-ID are returned.

If empty or "", messages with any Message-ID are returned.

parameters.name: filter.emailRecipient
parameters.description:
If given, only emails with the given email address in recipients are returned.

If empty or "", emails for all the recipients are included in the response.

0 - 100 characters

parameters.name: filter.emailSender
parameters.description:
If given, only emails from the given email address are returned.

If empty or "", emails from all the senders are included in the response.

0 - 100 characters

parameters.name: filter.emailSubject
parameters.description:
If given, only emails whose subject contains this string are returned.

If empty or "", emails with any subject are included in the response.

0 - 100 characters

parameters.name: filter.fileName
parameters.description:
If given, only quarantined files with the path terminated with this string are returned.

If empty or "", all files are returned.

parameters.name: filter.msSharepointRootSiteUuid
parameters.description:
Reference to [Microsoft Sharepoint root site].

If filled, only objects belonging to the given site or its child sites are returned.
If empty or "", objects of all sites are returned.

type: eset.dotnod.quarantine_management.v1-alpha.MicrosoftSharepointSite

parameters.name: filter.msTeamsTeamUuid
parameters.description:
Reference to [Microsoft Teams team].

type: eset.dotnod.quarantine_management.v1-alpha.MicrosoftTeamsTeam

parameters.name: filter.objectOrigin
parameters.description:
Origin of the quarantined object.

If filled, only objects with the given origin are returned. Objects with any origin are returned otherwise.

 - OBJECT_ORIGIN_UNSPECIFIED: fallback
 - OBJECT_ORIGIN_MS_OFFICE365: Object originates in Microsoft Office 365.
 - OBJECT_ORIGIN_GOOGLE_WORKSPACE: Object originates in Google Workspace.
 - OBJECT_ORIGIN_DEVICE: Object originates from a device.

parameters.name: filter.objectType
parameters.description:
Type of quarantined object to be returned in the response.

If 0 or empty, all types of quarantined objects are returned.

 - QUARANTINED_OBJECT_TYPE_UNSPECIFIED: fallback
 - QUARANTINED_OBJECT_TYPE_EMAIL_MESSAGE: The object is an email message.
 - QUARANTINED_OBJECT_TYPE_EMAIL_ATTACHMENT: The object is an email attachment.
 - QUARANTINED_OBJECT_TYPE_FILE_ON_DRIVE: The object is a file on the drive.

parameters.name: filter.quarantineReason
parameters.description:
If given, only objects quarantined for this reason are returned.

If 0 or empty, objects quarantined for all possible reasons are returned.

 - QUARANTINE_REASON_UNSPECIFIED: fallback
 - QUARANTINE_REASON_MALWARE: The object was identified as malware or as containing malware.
 - QUARANTINE_REASON_GRAYWARE: The object was identified as grayware or as containing grayware. Grayware refers to potentially unwanted programs or files that, while not explicitly malicious, can negatively impact system performance or user experience.
 - QUARANTINE_REASON_PHISHING: The object (mostly email) was identified as phishing.
 - QUARANTINE_REASON_SPAM: The object (mostly email) was identified as unsolicited or spam.
 - QUARANTINE_REASON_SENDER_SPOOFING: The email's sender was identified as spoofed.
 - QUARANTINE_REASON_RULE: The object was quarantined by a rule (for example, `mail transfer protection rule`).

parameters.name: filter.quarantineTime.startTime
parameters.description:
Optional. Inclusive start of the interval.

If specified, a Timestamp matching this interval will have to be the same
or after the start.

parameters.name: filter.quarantineTime.endTime
parameters.description:
Optional. Exclusive end of the interval.

If specified, a Timestamp matching this interval will have to be before the
end.

parameters.name: filter.userUuid
parameters.description:
This is a reference to the user associated with the quarantined object through ownership of its storage.

If filled for a file, only the files on a cloud drive owned by the given user are included in the response.

If filled for mail, only the files in a mailbox owned by the given user are included in the response.

If null or empty, quarantined objects associated with any user and accessible to the caller will be included in the response.

type: user_management.v1.User

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description:
Limit for pagination purposes.

If unspecified or 0, the default value is 50. The maximum value is 1000; values above 1000 will be coerced to 1000.

> **Info:**
> For more information, refer to [Paginating Requests in APIs](https://medium.com/@ignaciochiazzo/paginating-requests-in-apis-d4883d4c1c4c)
>
> or [Design Patterns: Pagination](https://google.aip.dev/158)

parameters.name: pageToken
parameters.description:
Page token of the current page.

If not given or "", the first page is returned.

> **Info:**
> For more information, refer to [Paginating Requests in APIs](https://medium.com/@ignaciochiazzo/paginating-requests-in-apis-d4883d4c1c4c)
>
> or [Design Patterns: Pagination](https://google.aip.dev/158)

parameters.name: orderBy
parameters.description:
Comma-separated list of fields (from the request resource).

Allowed formats are:
- [snake_case version of the field name](https://developers.google.com/protocol-buffers/docs/style#message_and_field_names) as used by protocol buffers.
- [JSON version of the field name](https://protobuf.dev/programming-guides/proto3/#json)

The syntax is described at [Standard Methods: List Ordering](https://google.aip.dev/132#ordering)

contentType.description: The HTTP Content-Type header value specifying the content type of the body.

data.description: The HTTP request/response body as raw binary.

extensions.description:
Application specific response metadata. Must be set in the first response
for streaming APIs.

@type.description:
A URL/resource name that uniquely identifies the type of the serialized
protocol buffer message. This string must contain at least
one "/" character. The last segment of the URL's path must represent
the fully qualified name of the type (as in
`path/google.protobuf.Duration`). The name should be in a canonical form
(e.g., leading "." is not accepted).

In practice, teams usually precompile into the binary all types that they
expect it to use in the context of Any. However, for URLs which use the
scheme `http`, `https`, or no scheme, one can optionally set up a type
server that maps type URLs to message definitions as follows:

* If no scheme is provided, `https` is assumed.
* An HTTP GET on the URL must yield a [google.protobuf.Type][]
  value in binary format, or produce an error.
* Applications are allowed to cache lookup results based on the
  URL, or have them precompiled into a binary to avoid any
  lookup. Therefore, binary compatibility needs to be preserved
  on changes to types. (Use versioned type names to manage
  breaking changes.)

Note: this functionality is not currently available in the official
protobuf release, and it is not used for type URLs beginning with
type.googleapis.com. As of May 2023, there are no widely used type server
implementations and no plans to implement one.

Schemes other than `http`, `https` (or the empty scheme) might be
used with implementation specific semantics.

startTime.description:
Optional. Inclusive start of the interval.

If specified, a Timestamp matching this interval will have to be the same
or after the start.

endTime.description:
Optional. Exclusive end of the interval.

If specified, a Timestamp matching this interval will have to be before the
end.

typeInterval.description:
Represents a time interval, encoded as a Timestamp start (inclusive) and a
Timestamp end (exclusive).

The start must be less than or equal to the end.
When the start equals the end, the interval is empty (matches no time).
When both start and end are unspecified, the interval matches any time.

objectUuids.description:
Reference to [quarantined object]s to be deleted.

type: QuarantinedObject

Reference to [quarantined object]s to be downloaded.

type: QuarantinedObject

addToExclusions.description: If true, objects will be excluded from scanning and will not end up in the quarantine again.

objectUuids.description:
Reference to [quarantined object]s to be restored.

type: QuarantinedObject

excludedObjectUuids.description:
Reference to [quarantined object]s excluded from the result.

type: QuarantinedObject

bodyParts.description: Body of the email is composed from parts (Content-Type: `multipart/*`), each of which can be treated as a file.

cc.description: Carbon copy recipient(s) of the email.

internetMessageId.title: Unique identifier of the message according to https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Message-ID

isReadOnly.description: True if the scanned object was read-only and cannot be modified/cleaned/deleted.

mailboxUserUuid.description:
Reference to the user who owns the mailbox, if the email can be associated with a mailbox.

If the email cannot be associated with a mailbox, this will be empty.

type: user_management.v1.User

reference.description:
How the email is referred to. Emails can be sourced in various systems with different types of indexing (e.g. URI, UUID, etc.).

For referencing this email within this API the `uuid` is used.

Object reference must be unique within one parent object (e,g, email or archive) to ensure referential integrity or the scope of protection (e.g. one account).

This value is not required to be URL-safe.

For example:
- Outlook email can be referenced by EntryID (or SearchKey)
- Exchange email can be referenced by combination of `Timestamp` | `Subject` | `Sender` fields.

> **Info:**
> Optimally, the reference should be universally unique identifier. That prevents from ambiguities during pairing of the object with scan results with actions. 
> 
> This might, however, be substantially difficult to achieve in distributed email-system, where the id-issuing authority is out of our reach and might be spoofed by e.g. spammers.

senderIpAddress.description: IP address of the sender. Might be IPv4 or IPv6.

uuid.description:
Unique identifier of the entity.

Must be collision-free - two identifiers created anywhere in the world must not collide within the entity parent scope. Unless a member of aggregate, the entity scope is always global.

Although most of the times compliant with [RFC 9562: A Universally Unique IDentifier (UUID) URN Namespace](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc9562), do not rely on it being a RFC UUID. Treat it as an opaque identifier, albeit of predictable length and alphabet.

RFC UUID can be recognized by being formatted according to the template xxxxxxxx-xxxx-Mxxx-Nxxx-xxxxxxxxxxxx, as explained on [Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Universally_unique_identifier#Format).

UUID is used for referencing an entity, even across domains.

Example: "123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000"

containedFiles.description:
List of files contained within this file, in case this file has internal structure (e.g. it is an archive).

Files contained in archive can have their `storages` empty, as they implicitly follow from the parent's storage.

hashSha1.description: [SHA-1 (Secure Hash Algorithm 1)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/SHA-1) digest of file's content.

hashSha2256.description: [SHA-2 256 (Secure Hash Algorithm 2)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/SHA-2) 256 digest of file's content.

isReadOnly.description: True if the scanned object was read-only and cannot be modified/cleaned/deleted.

path.description:
File path. Absolute path is absolute within storage.

For example:
`/documents/trip.xml` might be absolute within Microsoft Sharepoint Site.

reference.description:
How the file is referred to. Files can be hosted in various systems with different types of indexing (e.g. URI, UUID, etc.).

Object reference must be unique within one parent object (e,g, email or archive) to ensure referential integrity.

Optimally, the reference should be universally unique identifier. That prevents from ambiguities during pairing of the object with scan results with actions.

storages.description:
An array that represents the various storage locations associated with a file.

Each element in the array is an object that represents a different storage location. This could include email, archive, Microsoft Sharepoint site, Hyper-V Volume, etc. 

The order of the elements in the array represents the hierarchy of storage locations. For example, a storages array of ["email", "archive"] indicates that the file is first associated with an email, and within that email, it’s stored in an archive.

v1File.description:
Describes file.

File can be contained within other structures, such as email (attachment), archive or Miscrosoft Sharepoint Site. Those structures form `parents` of the file.

displayName.description:
Human readable name of the storage.

For example, a name of Microsoft Teams Team or name the archive file.

msSharepointRootSiteUuid.description:
Reference to [Microsoft Sharepoint root site].

Only valid if the object originates in Sharepoint site.

type: quarantine_management.v1.MicrosoftSharepointSite

uuid.description: Unique identifier of the file storage instance where the file resides. Represents the connected cloud or on-premises storage system (e.g., a specific OneDrive, Google Drive, or enterprise file share).

archiveReference.description: Reference to the archive containing the file. Can be an URL or path to the parent archive.

cloudDriveUserUuid.description:
If the file resides in the cloud on a cloud drive (for example, Google Drive or Microsoft OneDrive), this attribute references the user who owns that drive.

type: user_management.v1.User

emailReference.description:
Reference to the email containing the file.

The file itself can be part of the email body, an email attachment, or part of an attached archive.

v1FileStorage.description:
File storage represents a different storage location. This could include email, archive, Microsoft Sharepoint Site, Hyper-V Volume, etc.

File path is relative to this storage.

objectCount.description: Count of quarantined objects matching the criteria in the filter.

objects.description: List of quarantined objects matching the filter.

nextPageToken.description:
Page token of the next page.

Empty or "" for the last page.

> **Info:**
> For more information, refer to [Paginating Requests in APIs](https://medium.com/@ignaciochiazzo/paginating-requests-in-apis-d4883d4c1c4c)
>
> or [Design Patterns: Pagination](https://google.aip.dev/158)

hello.description:
Parameter of extended HELLO (`EHLO`) or HELLO (`HELO`) command.

> **Info:**
> For details of the command see: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc5321#section-4.1.1.1

recipients.description:
Parameter of RECIPIENT (`RCPT`) command or multiple commands for multiple recipients. Every `RCPT` command is listed separately.

> **Info:**
> For details of the command see: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc5321#section-4.1.1.3 .

sender.description:
Parameter (reverse-path) of MAIL (`MAIL FROM`) command. A sender of the email.

> **Info:**
> For details of the command see: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc5321#section-4.1.1.2

senderIpAddress.description: IP address of the sender. Might be IPv4 or IPv6.

v1MailTransferAgentSmtpDetails.description:
Details of SMTP activity at Mail Transfer Agent (MTA).

> **Info:**
> For MTA specification see: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc5321#section-2.3.3

v1ObjectEditor.description: Details of user who edited an object.

v1ObjectOrigin.description:
Object origin denotes a system managing object's life-cycle.

This is necessary to be known for actions that manipulate with object, such as delete, quarantine, move and so on.

 - OBJECT_ORIGIN_UNSPECIFIED: fallback
 - OBJECT_ORIGIN_MS_OFFICE365: Object originates in Microsoft Office 365.
 - OBJECT_ORIGIN_GOOGLE_WORKSPACE: Object originates in Google Workspace.
 - OBJECT_ORIGIN_DEVICE: Object originates from a device.

v1ObjectScanStatus.description:
Defines possible statuses of object scan.

 - OBJECT_SCAN_STATUS_UNSPECIFIED: fallback
 - OBJECT_SCAN_STATUS_NOT_SCANNED: The object was not scanned for various reasons.

- some objects are not suitable for certain scans (for example, file is not suitable for spam scan)
- scan was not performed due to a failure
- the object was excluded from the classification explicitly by exclusions.
 - OBJECT_SCAN_STATUS_POSITIVE_RESULT: The object was scanned with a positive result.

This means:
- the spam was detected in an email scan.
- phishing was detected in a phishing scan.
- malware was detected in a malware scan, and so on.
 - OBJECT_SCAN_STATUS_NEGATIVE_RESULT: The object was scanned with a negative result, which means the object is clean.

excludedObjectUuids.description:
Reference to [quarantined object]s excluded from the result.

type: QuarantinedObject

cloudOfficeTenantUuid.description:
Reference to [cloud office tenant].

If filled, only objects of the referred tenant are returned. If empty or null, objects of any tenant are returned.

type: cloud_office_protection.v1.CloudOfficeTenant

emailInternetMessageId.description:
If filled, only messages with this Message-ID are returned.

If empty or "", messages with any Message-ID are returned.

emailRecipient.description:
If given, only emails with the given email address in recipients are returned.

If empty or "", emails for all the recipients are included in the response.

0 - 100 characters

emailSender.description:
If given, only emails from the given email address are returned.

If empty or "", emails from all the senders are included in the response.

0 - 100 characters

emailSubject.description:
If given, only emails whose subject contains this string are returned.

If empty or "", emails with any subject are included in the response.

0 - 100 characters

fileName.description:
If given, only quarantined files with the path terminated with this string are returned.

If empty or "", all files are returned.

msSharepointRootSiteUuid.description:
Reference to [Microsoft Sharepoint root site].

If filled, only objects belonging to the given site or its child sites are returned.
If empty or "", objects of all sites are returned.

type: eset.dotnod.quarantine_management.v1-alpha.MicrosoftSharepointSite

userUuid.description:
This is a reference to the user associated with the quarantined object through ownership of its storage.

If filled for a file, only the files on a cloud drive owned by the given user are included in the response.

If filled for mail, only the files in a mailbox owned by the given user are included in the response.

If null or empty, quarantined objects associated with any user and accessible to the caller will be included in the response.

type: user_management.v1.User

v1QuarantineFilter.description:
Quarantine filter.

Every entry can be seen as a condition.

If multiple conditions are filled, they are used in a filtering expression with the AND operator (that is, they must be fulfilled simultaneously).

v1QuarantineReason.description:
Reasons for the object being quarantined.

 - QUARANTINE_REASON_UNSPECIFIED: fallback
 - QUARANTINE_REASON_MALWARE: The object was identified as malware or as containing malware.
 - QUARANTINE_REASON_GRAYWARE: The object was identified as grayware or as containing grayware. Grayware refers to potentially unwanted programs or files that, while not explicitly malicious, can negatively impact system performance or user experience.
 - QUARANTINE_REASON_PHISHING: The object (mostly email) was identified as phishing.
 - QUARANTINE_REASON_SPAM: The object (mostly email) was identified as unsolicited or spam.
 - QUARANTINE_REASON_SENDER_SPOOFING: The email's sender was identified as spoofed.
 - QUARANTINE_REASON_RULE: The object was quarantined by a rule (for example, `mail transfer protection rule`).

displayName.description: Human-readable name of the quarantined object.

isExcludable.description: True if the object can be excluded from further scanning.

isRestorable.description: True if the object can be restored to its original location.

quarantineTime.description: Time when the object was quarantined.

scanResults.description:
Results of various scans of the quarantined object or objects contained within the quarantined object.

There is no limit to the number or types of scan results.

Possible scan result types for files:
- scan_management.v1.FileScanStatus
- threat_detection.v1.FileScanResult

Possible scan result types for emails:
- scan_management.v1.EmailScanStatus
- threat_detection.v1.SpamEmailScanResult
- threat_detection.v1.PhishingEmailScanResult

sizeBytes.description: Size of the quarantined object in bytes.

uuid.description:
Unique identifier of the entity.

Must be collision-free - two identifiers created anywhere in the world must not collide within entity parent scope. Unless a member of aggregate, the entity scope is always global.

Although most of the times compliant with [RFC 4122: A Universally Unique IDentifier (UUID) URN Namespace](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc4122), do not rely on it being a RFC UUID. Treat it as an opaque identifier.

RFC UUID can be recognized by being formatted according to the template xxxxxxxx-xxxx-Mxxx-Nxxx-xxxxxxxxxxxx, as explained on [Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Universally_unique_identifier#Format).

UUID is used for referencing an entity, even across domains.

Example: "123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000"

v1QuarantinedObjectType.description:
Possible types of an object.

 - QUARANTINED_OBJECT_TYPE_UNSPECIFIED: fallback
 - QUARANTINED_OBJECT_TYPE_EMAIL_MESSAGE: The object is an email message.
 - QUARANTINED_OBJECT_TYPE_EMAIL_ATTACHMENT: The object is an email attachment.
 - QUARANTINED_OBJECT_TYPE_FILE_ON_DRIVE: The object is a file on the drive.

displayName.description:
Human readable name of the rule.

> **Note:**
> Rule display names are not unique.
>
> This value is only a hint and might be ambiguous in some cases 
> (for example, two adminstrators unaware of each other set up two policies with equal rule names).

v1QuarantiningRule.description: Rule that caused quarantining of the object in case the reason == RULE.

addToExclusions.description: If true, objects will be excluded from scanning and will not end up in the quarantine again.

excludedObjectUuids.description:
Reference to [quarantined object]s excluded from the result.

type: QuarantinedObject

Bearer.description: Type "Bearer " and then your API Token
Bearer.name: Authorization
